rag_rules: Place component type contracts (assumptions, guarantees) in `annex agree { ... };` blocks within the component type.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use the AADL model as the authoritative source for component structure (names, ports, types).
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type guarantees.
Use case-provided bounds and visible feature names for range assumptions and guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq <variable_name> : bool = <expression>;` to define Boolean variables for range checks.
Ensure AGREE expression types match AADL port types (bool, int, real).
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not encode AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) directly in AGREE expressions without corresponding ports or constants.
Avoid range comparisons or equality checks on Boolean or opaque data ports unless a numeric scalar representation exists.
